title: Nozzle for a firewater monitor and a firewater monitor comprising such nozzle
abstract: The invention relates to a nozzle (100) for a firewater monitor (200), the nozzle (100) being configured for being coupled to an inlet piping (212) having an inlet flow area (212fa), the nozzle (100) having an outlet (115). The outlet has an outlet flow area (115fa). The nozzle (100) is configured for having a standby mode (SBM) and a throttle mode (TM) in operational use, wherein, in the standby mode (SBM), the outlet flow area (115fa) has a size similar to the inlet flow area (212fa). The nozzle (100) is configured for gradually throttling the outlet (115) when, in the throttle mode (TM) in operational use, fluid is flowing through the outlet (115). The nozzle of the invention reduces water surge pressures when the firewater nozzle is taken into operational use. The invention further relates to a firewater monitor comprising the nozzle of the invention.
claims:
Claims C l a i m s 1 . Nozzle (100) for a firewater monitor (200), the nozzle (100) being configured for being coupled to an inlet piping (212) having an inlet flow area (212fa), the nozzle (100) having an outlet (1 15), wherein the outlet has an outlet flow area (1 15fa), c h a r a c t e r i s e d i n that the nozzle (100) is configured for having a standby mode (SBM) and a throttle mode (TM) in operational use, wherein, in the standby mode (SBM), the outlet flow area (1 15fa) has a size similar to the inlet flow area (212fa), and wherein the nozzle (100) is configured for gradually throttling the outlet (1 15) when, in the throttle mode (TM) in operational use, fluid is flowing through the outlet (1 15). 2. Nozzle (100) according to claim 1 , wherein the nozzle (100) is configured for using pressure of the fluid in the nozzle (100) for gradually throttling the outlet (1 15) in the throttle mode (TM). 3. Nozzle (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein, in the standby mode (SBM) of the nozzle (100), the outlet flow area (1 15fa) has a size, which is between 80% and 90% of the inlet flow area (212fa). 4. Nozzle (100) according to claim 1 , 2 or 3, wherein, in the throttle mode (TM) the outlet flow area has a minimum size (115fa') between 10% and 20% of the inlet flow area (212fa). 5. Nozzle (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the nozzle (100) is provided with an expandable chamber (105), which is in fluid communication with the outlet (1 15) such that an increasing pressure in the outlet (1 15) leads to the fluid gradually filling and expanding the expandable chamber (105) to obtain an expansion thereof, wherein the nozzle (100) is further configured such that the expansion of the expandable-chamber (105) controls a reduction of the outlet flow area (1 15fa) for throttling the outlet (1 15). 6. Nozzle (100) according to claim 5, wherein the expandable chamber (105) is resiliency loaded in that it tends to go back to a smaller volume when the pressure in the outlet (1 15) drops. 7. Nozzle (100) according to claim 6, wherein the nozzle (100) is provided with a first hollow member (100-1 ) and a second hollow member (100-2) placed one within the other and being movable relative to each other, which form a variable 

 length telescopic assembly (1 OOts), wherein the expandable chamber (105) is defined by an annular space formed in between said hollow members (100-1 , 100- 2) when said hollow members (100-1 , 100-2) move away from each other. 8. Nozzle (100) according to claim 7, wherein the expandable chamber (105) is spring loaded by a plurality of springs (107) distributed around the circumference of the nozzle (100) and arranged in between the first hollow member (100-1 ) and the second hollow member (100-2). 9. Nozzle (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the nozzle (100) is provided with an axially-placed nozzle baffle (1 10) at a downstream end (100ds) of the nozzle (100) such that the outlet flow area (1 15fa) is defined by the annular space (1 15fa, 1 15fa') between the axially-placed nozzle baffle member (1 15) and an inner wall of the outlet (1 15) at the downstream end (1 OOds), wherein the axially-placed nozzle baffle (1 10) is configured for redirecting the fluid from a centre region to the outside. 10. Nozzle (100) according to claim 9, wherein the nozzle (100) is further provided with a telescopic jet head (100-3) at the downstream end (1 OOds) of the nozzle (100), wherein the telescopic jet head (100-3) is configured for cooperating with the axially-placed nozzle baffle (1 10) to form a fluid jet beam (JB) in operational use when being extended. 1 1 . Firewater monitor (200) comprising the nozzle (100) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims. 12. The firewater monitor (200) according to claim 1 1 , further comprising: a firewater monitor body (210) for being mounted on a feed pipe flange (310), and a fixing flange arrangement (205) for fixing the firewater monitor (200) onto the feed pipe flange (310). 13. The firewater monitor (200) according to claim 12, further comprising a joint assembly (220) provided in the firewater monitor body (210) for allowing directing the firewater monitor (200) to a specific fire location. 14. The firewater monitor (200) according to claim 13, wherein the joint (220) comprises: a first full bore swivel joint (220-1 ) provided in the firewater monitor body 

 (210) for allowing horizontal rotation of the firewater monitor (200), and a second full bore swivel joint (220-2) provided in the firewater monitor body (210) for allowing vertical rotation of the firewater monitor (200). A firewater system comprising a piping end section (300) for releasing firewater for extinguishing fires and a nozzle (100) in accordance with any one of claim 1 to 10, wherein the nozzle (100) is provided at the end of the piping end section (300).
